scenario_name: Scenario 11 — Queue Backpressure and Consumer Lag
justification: All 7 pass criteria met: (1) Throughput gap clearly identified as 300/min inbound vs 15-20/min processing with ~280 msgs/min accumulation math. (2) Unbounded backlog consequence described explicitly, including eventual SQS message expiration at 4-day retention. (3) Visibility timeout double-processing risk identified with arithmetic (10 × 3-4s = 30-40s > 30s timeout) and concrete mitigation options. (4) Missing DLQ identified as 'MUST FIX' blocker and escalated to Coach K. (5) Queue depth metric gap called out as 'CRITICAL' — the single most important missing metric. (6) Autoscaling recommended with specific worker count calculation (300 ÷ 18 ≈ 17 workers). (7) Verdict is NOT READY.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough throughput analysis with specific accumulation rate (~280 msgs/min) and consequence timeline
Visibility timeout arithmetic is precise and includes multiple mitigation strategies (increase timeout, per-message delete, heartbeating, idempotency)
Goes beyond rubric requirements: identifies single point of failure, missing circuit breakers, misleading /health endpoint, missing graceful shutdown, and downstream dependency risks
Escalations are well-structured with clear routing and reasoning
Confidence section honestly flags ambiguities (parallelism model, idempotency guarantees) rather than overclaiming certainty
Operational readiness section includes concrete infrastructure provisioning requirements (DLQ, RedrivePolicy, CloudWatch alarms, autoscaling group)

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: throughput gap clearly identified (300/min vs 15-20/min), unbounded backlog consequence described, visibility timeout double-processing risk identified with specific math (10×4s=40s > 30s timeout), missing DLQ identified as a blocker, queue depth metric gap called out as critically insufficient, autoscaling recommended (15-20 worker instances or concurrency increase), verdict is NOT READY.

observations.text: Throughput math is precise and well-articulated: 280 jobs/min deficit, 16,000+ backlog within 1 hour
Visibility timeout analysis goes beyond identification — explains the exact race condition with batch timing (10×4s=40s vs 30s timeout)
DLQ absence framed as a blocker with clear explanation of poison message cycling
Queue depth metric gap explicitly called out as 'the single most important indicator' missing
Autoscaling recommendation is specific: 15-20x capacity needed, with both horizontal (more instances) and vertical (concurrency) options
Escalations are well-structured and routed appropriately
Goes beyond rubric requirements with additional failure modes: worker crash mid-batch, downstream dependency failures, OOM on large images, graceful shutdown, circuit breakers
Observability review is exceptionally thorough — identifies missing correlation IDs, latency histograms, error type breakdowns, and the false-health endpoint problem

trials.justification: All seven pass criteria met: (1) Throughput gap explicitly calculated as 300/min inbound vs 15-20/min processing with 15-20x shortfall noted. (2) Unbounded backlog consequence described — queue depth grows by ~280 jobs/min during peak. (3) Visibility timeout double-processing risk identified with detailed math (10 × 2-4s = 20-40s > 30s timeout). (4) Missing DLQ identified as CRITICAL blocker with escalation to Coach K. (5) Queue depth metric gap called out — ApproximateNumberOfMessagesVisible missing, operators cannot see backlog. (6) Autoscaling recommended (ECS/K8s HPA) with specific sizing (15-20 worker instances), plus alternative of adding concurrency within the worker. (7) Verdict is NOT READY.

observations.text: Throughput gap math is precise and includes the delta (280 jobs/min growth rate), not just the two rates
Visibility timeout analysis goes beyond identification — provides three concrete mitigations (increase timeout, reduce batch size, ChangeMessageVisibility heartbeat)
DLQ absence framed as CRITICAL blocker with specific maxReceiveCount recommendation (3-5)
Identifies secondary risks beyond the rubric: idempotency gap, memory exhaustion from large images, missing circuit breakers, no graceful shutdown
Escalations are well-structured with clear infrastructure_gap typing and routing to Coach K
Observability review is thorough — identifies missing queue depth, duration histograms, redelivery counts, duplicate counters, and correlation IDs
Confidence section is honest at 55% with clear separation of deterministic facts vs inferred risks
